rationale: The citing paper note centers on multilevel effects of manufacturer training and spiffs on downstream salesperson effort/performance in B2B distributor channels, whereas the cited paper note is a macro time-series study of aggregate customer satisfaction (ACSI) and U.S. discretionary spending growth. There is no overlap in focal constructs or methods indicated in the notes, and the match evidence is only a bare DOI reference (no citation context), which suggests this is likely an incidental/background citation rather than a substantive dependency.
